evals.prompt: Read Docs/Specs/Inventory.md and create the implementation TODO and test case list for it.
evals.expected_output: Creates Docs/Specs/Inventory-todo.md with Todo and Test cases sections only, includes Logic/UI/Data categorized tasks, and does not modify the source spec.
expectations: The skill reads the referenced spec before writing output.
The output file is written in the same folder as the spec using the <spec-stem>-todo.md naming pattern.
Todo items use [Logic], [UI], or [Data] category tags.
Test cases map to the meaningful requirements and edge states in the spec.
No implementation code or source spec changes are made.

evals.prompt: Turn the shop UI spec into todos and tests. The spec is at Docs/Specs/Shop_UI.md.
evals.expected_output: Investigates existing shop/UI code and produces a same-folder TODO document that includes UI/UX interaction, empty/error/loading state, accessibility, and relevant logic/data tasks with matching tests.
expectations: The skill performs codebase investigation before finalizing tasks.
The generated TODO includes UI/UX states required by the spec without inventing unrelated redesign work.
Tasks are split by responsibility instead of combining UI, logic, and data in one item.
Test cases include user-flow and edge-state verification.

evals.prompt: goal-todo for the latest spec in Docs/Specs; make the checklist actionable for implementation.
evals.expected_output: Finds likely spec candidates, asks for clarification if multiple plausible specs exist, then creates an actionable same-folder TODO/test checklist once the input spec is resolved.
expectations: The skill scans likely spec locations when no exact path is provided.
The skill asks for a specific spec when multiple plausible files exist.
The final checklist contains only implementable tasks and verification cases, not broad planning prose.
